# sndwrks

> sndwrks is a live entertainment command center platform combining server hardware and software to route show-control messages, monitor atmospherics, fire macros, and coordinate departments over a unified network.

sndwrks local is the core product: a physical server that sits at the center of your show network, paired with a browser-based web app for configuration and control. It supports OSC (UDP and TCP), sACN, message routing, haze watch, macros, chat, and a full OSC API for programmatic control.

[Welcome](https://docs.sndwrks.com/): Complete documentation home page with quick links to hardware setup, software setup, products, guides, and reference.

## Products

- [Overview](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/overview): Entry point for all product documentation with links to hardware and software quick starts.
- [sndwrks local](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/home): Overview of the sndwrks local server product covering both hardware and software components.
- [Server Hardware: Getting Started](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/server-hardware/getting-started): Home page for server hardware documentation linking to what it is and quick setup.
- [Server Hardware: What is it?](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/server-hardware/what-is-it): Describes the physical server, its role at the center of a show network, port types (power, network, peripherals), and redundancy recommendations.
- [Server Hardware: Quick Setup](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/server-hardware/quick-setup): Step-by-step unboxing, inspection, and power-on instructions for both the server and server smol hardware variants.
- [Software: Getting Started](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/getting-started): Index page linking to all software documentation sections including devices, router, macros, haze watch, chat, utility, and settings.
- [Software: What is it?](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/what-is-it): High-level description of the browser-based web app and why a browser interface was chosen for show environments.
- [Software: Quick Setup](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/quick-setup): Guided walkthrough to get the server app running in a browser, configure an IP address, and add the first devices to the system.
- [Software: Overview](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/overview): Documents the show-floor home page and how to pin macro shortcut buttons for one-tap firing.
- [Software: Devices](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/devices): Explains how to add, configure, enable, and delete network devices (consoles, processors, sensors, etc.) that serve as building blocks for routing, macros, and haze watch.
- [Software: Router](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/router): Documents the message router and sACN router, including crosspoint configuration, filters, universe mapping, and message history audit trail.
- [Software: Macros](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/macros): Covers the node-based macro system including triggers (OSC, sACN, server event, wall clock), commands (device, OSC, TCP, UDP, HTTP, email, system), and transformers (delay, rate limit).
- [Software: Haze Watch](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/haze-watch): Documents atmospheric monitoring using particulate, temperature, humidity, and CO₂ sensors, with trigger and command setup for automated haze control via OSC.
- [Software: Chat](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/chat): Describes the show-team chat feature currently in preview, including sign-in and notification settings.
- [Software: Utility](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/utility): Covers the built-in network scanner, event log, and syslog viewer for troubleshooting and device discovery.
- [Software: Settings](https://docs.sndwrks.com/products/sndwrks-local/software/settings): Documents all server configuration options including power controls, network settings, e-stop, double-message protection, sACN, syslog, chat notifications, and backup/restore.

## Guides

- [Using Code to Send OSC](https://docs.sndwrks.com/guides/using-code-to-send-osc): Practical guide with working Python and Node.js scripts for sending OSC messages from the terminal, including setup with pyenv and nvm, and a real-world login-item automation example.
- [UDP vs TCP](https://docs.sndwrks.com/guides/udp-vs-tcp): Explains the difference between UDP and TCP transport protocols, when to use each in show-control contexts, and how the TCP handshake and retransmission work under the hood.

## Reference

- [OSC API V1](https://docs.sndwrks.com/reference/osc-api-v1): Complete OSC command reference for sndwrks local covering global commands (confetti, ping, e-stop), devices, router crosspoints, events, haze watch triggers, macros, and chat messaging.
- [AI Statement](https://docs.sndwrks.com/reference/ai-statement): Describes how sndwrks uses AI (primarily Claude) to assist with documentation generation and proofreading, with notes on human review practices.
